{"problem_standardized_math":"Suppose that, for each residual function $r_j$, both $r_j$ and $\nabla r_j$ are Lipschitz continuous on a compact set $\\mathcal{D} \\subset \\mathbf{R}^n$ with Lipschitz constant $L$, that is,\n\\[\n|r_j(x)-r_j(\\tilde{x})| \\le L\\|x-\\tilde{x}\\|, \\qquad \\|\\nabla r_j(x)-\\nabla r_j(\\tilde{x})\\| \\le L\\|x-\\tilde{x}\\|\n\\]\nfor all $j=1,2,\\ldots,m$ and all $x,\\tilde{x}\\in\\mathcal{D}$. Assume also that the residuals are bounded on $\\mathcal{D}$, i.e., there exists $M>0$ such that $|r_j(x)|\\le M$ for all $j=1,2,\\ldots,m$ and all $x\\in\\mathcal{D}$. Let\n\\[\nJ(x)=\\left[\\frac{\\partial r_j}{\\partial x_i}\\right]_{\\substack{j=1,2,\\ldots,m\\\\ i=1,2,\\ldots,n}}\n=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\nabla r_1(x)^T\\\\\n\\nabla r_2(x)^T\\\\\n\\vdots\\\\\n\\nabla r_m(x)^T\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\nabla f(x)=\\sum_{j=1}^m r_j(x)\\nabla r_j(x)=J(x)^T r(x).\n\\]\nFind Lipschitz constants for the Jacobian $J$ and the gradient $\\nabla f$ on $\\mathcal{D}$."}
